stories.headline: Investors pile into clean energy as Iran war drives push for energy security
stories.summary: Renewables funds attract biggest flows for five years, as focus shifts from climate change to geopolitical crisis

stories.headline: Iran war: What’s happening on day 65 as Trump reviews new plan to end war?
stories.summary: Iran has sent the US a new 14-point proposal to end the war.

stories.headline: Trump expresses doubt that Iran’s peace proposal is ‘acceptable’
stories.summary: US president says he is considering the 14-point peace plan, but warns strikes could resume if Tehran 'misbehaves'.

stories.headline: Trump says Iran has 'not yet paid a big enough price' as he reviews new peace proposal
stories.summary: The US president had earlier in the week refused another Iranian proposal, saying Tehran is not willing to give Washington what it “needs to have” to strike a deal.

stories.headline: South Korea holds pro-Palestine protest in solidarity with Gaza
stories.summary: Protesters in South Korea’s capital, Seoul, were in solidarity with Palestinians in Gaza enduring genocidal attacks.

stories.headline: Germany troop cuts send wrong signal to Russia, say two top US Republicans
stories.summary: The chairs of the House and Senate armed services committees say withdrawing 5,000 service personnel risked undermining deterrence.

stories.headline: Millions of jobs lost as Iranians battle ‘Operation Economic Fury’
stories.summary: A fragile ceasefire may have paused the US-Israeli war on Iran, but the economic cost is crippling life in Tehran.

stories.headline: US approves $8.6bn in arms sales to Middle East allies
stories.summary: US State Department cites emergency circumstances to fast-track sales without the approval of Congress.

stories.headline: Iran war live: Trump to review 14-point plan; Israel pounds Lebanon
stories.summary: US president says he will 'soon be reviewing the plan Iran has just sent to us', but doesn't think he can make a deal.

stories.headline: Trump says US will reduce troop presence in Germany 'a lot further' than initial 5,000 withdrawal
stories.summary: Trump indicated without sharing details that a substantial further reduction of US troop presence in Germany was imminent, just a day after the Pentagon announced a withdrawal of 5,000 soldiers. Trump has also threatened earlier in the week to rollback troops in Spain and Italy.

stories.headline: OPEC+ Meeting Offers Chance to Show Unity After UAE’s Shock Exit
stories.summary: This weekend’s talks among OPEC+ nations provide the oil producer group with an opportunity to project unity after the shock departure of longtime member the United Arab Emirates.

stories.headline: Taiwan leader visits Eswatini despite China’s attempts to block trip
stories.summary: President William Lai Ching-te says he met King Mswati III and signed trade agreements.

stories.headline: Will Hormuz crisis spur Asia to boost oil reserve cooperation?
Takaichi Set for Australia Visit to Strengthen Japan Alliance
stories.summary: Japan’s prime minister is set to arrive in Australia to strengthen ties with one of her country’s strongest allies as she seeks to build on an updated regional strategy laid out in Vietnam.

stories.headline: Japan’s Katayama Declines to Comment on FX Intervention For Now
stories.summary: Japanese Finance Minister Satsuki Katayama declined to comment if authorities intervened to support the yen last week, after reports said they had entered the market for the first time since 2024.

stories.headline: In Mississippi, a Democrat Challenges the Senator Who Blocked His Judgeship
stories.summary: Scott Colom, a state prosecutor, is running against Senator Cindy Hyde-Smith, a Republican who denied him a seat on the federal bench. In a tough year for the G.O.P., Democrats see hope for his long-shot bid.

stories.headline: Man Shot by ICE in California Indicted on Assault Charges
stories.summary: Prosecutors have accused the man of hitting federal agents with his vehicle as they tried to arrest him.

stories.headline: Delayed Louisiana Primaries Stoke Confusion at Ballot Box
stories.summary: On the first day of early voting, some glimpses of voter bewilderment came into focus, part of a dizzying scramble playing out days after a major Supreme Court ruling.

stories.summary: David Farley says net overseas migration of 306,000 ‘probably not’ too much despite party policy to cap migrants at 130,000 per yearFollow our Australia news live blog for latest updatesGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastBarnaby Joyce has blamed “the pressure of a campaign” for One Nation’s Farrer candidate contradicting party policy on immigration and appearing to endorse Labor’s current intake.The One Nation MP also clai

stories.headline: Japan’s Inoue beats Nakatani to retain undisputed super bantamweight title
stories.summary: Naoya Inoue wins 116-112, 115-113, 116-112 in a fight billed as the biggest boxing match in ​Japanese history.

stories.headline: In Yemen, Starlink internet brings opportunities – for some
stories.summary: Despite Houthi resistance and affordability challenges, Starlink fuels growth in Yemen's digital workforce.

stories.headline: Carlos Prates punishes Jack Della Maddalena at UFC Fight Night Australia
stories.summary: Brazil's Prates crushes hometown hero Maddalena in the Perth fight; challenges the winner of Makhachev vs Garry.

stories.headline: To Lam meets Modi, ASEAN ministerial meeting, Sony's business strategy
Japan trade show highlights quality-of-life innovations for pets
Japan renews Indo-Pacific strategy, focusing on supply chain resilience
Takaichi's musical diplomacy with Indonesia shows fresh bid for Asia partners
From fridges to slow cookers: Can plug-in solar panels actually power our everyday appliances?
stories.summary: Plug-in solar is having its moment in the spotlight. But how much power can these mini devices actually generate?

stories.headline: Trump tells Congress ceasefire means he does not need their approval for Iran war
stories.summary: The president writes that hostilities "have terminated" because of the ceasefire, arguing he does not need congressional authorisation.

stories.headline: Two people killed and over a dozen injured in Russian attacks across Ukraine
stories.summary: Russia is continuing to bombard Ukrainian cities daily with barrages of drones and missiles as ceasefire talks appear to have stalled in the wake of the war in Iran. Kyiv says its counter strikes targeting Russian oil facilities have been achieving success.

stories.headline: The Iran war has strengthened Ukraine in surprising ways. Could a ceasefire with Russia be closer?
stories.summary: President Zelensky has been visiting the Gulf to demonstrate his country's military nous.

stories.headline: Thirteen killed in Israeli strikes on southern Lebanon, health ministry says
stories.summary: Four women and a child are among the dead, as fighting between Israel and Iran-backed Hezbollah continues despite a ceasefire.

stories.headline: Seven killed in southern Lebanon amid Israeli airstrikes and ceasefire tensions
stories.summary: State media reported that several villages were hit as neighbourhoods near the border are being levelled. The IDF maintains that 50 airstrikes conducted over 24 hours specifically targeted Hezbollah infrastructure.

stories.headline: Trump administration fast-tracks $8.6bn in arms sales to Middle East allies
stories.summary: Move to shore up regional partners during Iran war comes as US warns Europe of delays to weapons shipments

stories.headline: Berkshire cash pile climbs to $380bn as stock sales streak hits 14th quarter
stories.summary: Greg Abel to address shareholders at first gathering since he succeeded Warren Buffett

stories.headline: Robert Fico will back Ukraine's EU membership after call, Zelenskyy says
stories.summary: Zelenskyy said they had invited each other to meet. Slovakia's prime minister has backed Viktor Orbán's isolated line on Ukraine, though less fervently than the outgoing Hungarian leader.

stories.headline: Zambia cancels world’s largest human rights and tech summit days before start
stories.summary: Government blocks RightsCon 2026 conference saying it did not ‘align with national values’The world’s largest conference on human rights and technology has been cancelled just days before it was due to start after the Zambian government told organisers it did not align with “national values”.Zambia’s government had originally welcomed the RightsCon 2026 summit on “human rights in the digital age”, due to be held in the capital, Lusaka, on 5-8 May

stories.headline: Top Republicans express concern over withdrawal of US troops from Germany – as it happened
stories.summary: Senator Roger Wicker and representative Mike Rogers say move risks undermining deterrence and sending wrong signal to PutinFull report: Nato seeks to ‘understand the details’ after US decision to withdraw troops from Germany‘We love our Americans’: the German town rocked by Trump’s plan to withdraw US troopsThe UK Maritime Trade Operations (UKMTO) said suspicious activity had been reported 84 nautical miles southwest of the port of Mukalla in Yem

stories.headline: Spirit Airlines collapses amid rising fuel costs from war on Iran
stories.summary: US budget carrier Spirit Airlines shuts down after talks for a government bailout failed.

stories.headline: Concern for jailed Iranian Nobel laureate as family say health deteriorating
stories.summary: On Friday her family said the 54-year-old had been taken from prison to a local hospital after a sharp deterioration in her health.

stories.headline: Taiwan president visits Eswatini days after blaming China for cancelled trip
stories.summary: It is unclear how he reached Eswatini. China described his visit as a "stowaway-style escape farce".

stories.headline: Spirit Shuts Operations After White House Bailout Collapses
stories.summary: Spirit Aviation Holdings is winding down operations after the troubled US discount carrier buckled under the weight of surging fuel prices and a government bailout dangled by US President Donald Trump fell through. All Spirit flights have been canceled, and passengers have been advised not to go to the airport. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: UAE Departs OPEC as Iran Feel Economic Sting of Blockade
stories.summary: Exxon Mobil Corp. and Chevron Corp. posted stronger-than-expected earnings for the first quarter as higher oil and natural gas prices outweighed production outages from the Iran war. Bloomberg Opinion Energy and Commodities Columnist joins David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Spirit Ends Operations After White House Bailout Collapses
stories.summary: Spirit Aviation Holdings Inc. is winding down operations after the troubled US discount carrier buckled under the weight of surging fuel prices and a government bailout dangled by President Donald Trump fell through.

stories.headline: Berkshire Hathaway’s Cash Surges in Abel’s First Quarter as CEO
stories.summary: Berkshire Hathaway Inc.’s cash pile soared to its highest level ever and operating earnings jumped in Greg Abel’s first quarter as chief executive officer.

stories.headline: Mace, Boebert and Luna Want Congress Members to Pay a Price for Sexual Abuse. Will It Work?
stories.summary: Three Republican women who had a hand in forcing the resignations of lawmakers accused of sexual misconduct said they are out to name and shame more offenders. It is not clear how far they will go.

stories.headline: Cuba says Trump’s fresh sanctions on its economy amount to ‘collective punishment’
stories.summary: The US sanctions target people operating in broad sections of Cuban economy, including energy, defence and miningCuba’s government has said new sanctions imposed on the island by Donald Trump amounted to “collective punishment”, as an enormous 1 May procession outside the American embassy in Havana vowed to “defend the homeland”.In an executive order on Friday, the US president said he would impose sanctions on people involved in broad sections o

stories.headline: Oil Tanker Pricing Feud Embroils Centuries-Old London Market
stories.summary: A legal claim from one of the world’s largest oil traders is throwing a spotlight on an arcane but critical corner of global finance — the multibillion-dollar freight market and the 282-year-old City of London institution that sits at the heart of it.

stories.headline: Aegon, Barclays Say Prepare for Market Pain
stories.summary: Skeptics including Aegon Asset Management and Barclays Plc are getting ready for April’s credit rally to potentially vanish as quickly as it appeared.

stories.headline: Bloomberg This Weekend 5/02/2026
stories.summary: The news doesn’t stop when markets close. Hosts David Gura, Christina Ruffini and Lisa Mateo bring clarity, context and a bit of humor to the weekend’s biggest headlines, LIVE from New York. Joined by The Telegraph Senior Foreign Correspondent Adrian Blomfield, Actor Ben McKenzie, The Points Guy Managing Editor Clint Henderson and GasBuddy Head of Petroleum Analysis Patrick De Haan. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: OPEC+ Provisionally Agrees June Quota Increase, Delegate Says
stories.summary: Major OPEC+ nations came to a provisional agreement on another modest but symbolic increase to supply targets for June, in the group’s first move since the UAE’s surprise exit, a delegate said.

stories.headline: Supreme Court Asked to Restore Access to Abortion Pill by Mail
stories.summary: A federal appeals court temporarily halted a Food and Drug Administration regulation that has greatly expanded access to the abortion pill mifepristone.

stories.headline: Trump is running out of options to contain gas price backlash
stories.summary: The rising cost of oil is becoming a major political concern for the White House, as the stalemate between President Donald Trump and Iran drags on.

stories.headline: A deadline for the Iran war is here. What does the War Powers Act say?
stories.summary: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth asserted an ongoing ceasefire “pauses” the clock on the 60-day deadline to end hostilities in Iran or seek congressional approval.

stories.headline: It's not just oil: Iran war also threatens Asia's food security
stories.summary: Planting season is beginning in Asia - but the closure of the Strait of Hormuz and Chinese restrictions has hit fertiliser supply.

stories.headline: War criminal Mladic close to death, say lawyers asking judge for jail release
stories.summary: Mladic, 84, was jailed for life for genocide, war crimes and crimes against humanity during the Bosnian war in 1992-95.

stories.headline: Myanmar ex-leader Aung San Suu Kyi moved to house arrest, military says
stories.summary: The Nobel Peace Prize laureate has been in detention since she was ousted in a military coup in 2021.

stories.headline: Aung San Suu Kyi: The Myanmar democracy icon detained for years
stories.summary: Little has been heard of Myanmar's former leader since she was ousted in a military coup in 2021.

stories.headline: Pope appoints former undocumented immigrant as bishop of West Virginia
stories.summary: Leo, who has criticized Trump’s hardline immigration policy, selected Evelio Menjivar-Ayala as state’s new bishopSign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inboxPope Leo XIV has appointed a man who had once entered the United States as an undocumented immigrant, hidden in the trunk of a car, as the new bishop of West Virginia.The pope approved the resignation of Bishop Mark E Brennan of Wheeling-Charleston, West Virgi

stories.headline: Trump says he will hike tariffs on EU cars to 25%
stories.summary: The current level of tariffs charged on goods entering the US from the EU is 15%, under a deal negotiated last July.

stories.headline: China scraps tariffs for all but one African nation
stories.summary: The zero-tariff regime gives China's soft power a boost, but may lead to uneven gains, say analysts.

stories.headline: Columbia’s Outlook Revised to Negative as Trump Slams Colleges
stories.summary: Columbia University’s credit outlook was revised to negative by Moody’s Ratings, which cited rising risks tied to the “federal environment for higher education.”

stories.headline: Could Santa Marta climate talks mark ground zero in push to ditch fossil fuels?
stories.summary: Colombia hosted nearly 60 countries at pivotal time on world stage for fight to transition to a clean energy futureLooking out to sea from the grey sandy beaches of Santa Marta, on Colombia’s Caribbean coast, it is never hard to spot evidence of the country’s thriving fossil fuel export trade. Oil tankers ride at anchor on the horizon and sometimes, locals say, lumps of coal wash up on the shore, blown off the collier ships that carry cargos from

stories.headline: Venice Biennale jury resigns days before start of top art exhibition
stories.summary: It follows growing tensions over the return of Russia for the first time since the full-scale invasion of Ukraine.

stories.headline: Israel intercepts Gaza flotilla near Crete and detains 175 activists
stories.summary: Pro-Palestinian activists say 22 boats carrying aid for Gaza were illegally intercepted in international waters near the Greek island.

stories.headline: Belgium plans to nationalise nuclear power plants
stories.summary: Prime Minister Bart De Wever said the move would see "less dependence on fossil fuel imports and more control over our own supply".

stories.headline: Jewish agency warned of 'high' threat level ahead of Bondi shooting, report finds
stories.summary: Gun reforms should be prioritised and policing around Jewish events increased, royal commission says.

stories.headline: China to ban drone sales in Beijing citing security concerns
stories.summary: Drone users will also be required to register their devices and secure permits before flying them.

stories.headline: US charges Mexican governor and other leaders with aiding drug cartel
stories.summary: The governor of Sinaloa, Rubén Rocha Moya, hails from the same party as the president of Mexico.

stories.headline: Elon Musk and Sam Altman bring their rivalry to court
stories.summary: Of the two unloved billionaires, which will the jury trust?

stories.headline: Global forest loss slows but El Niño fires could threaten progress
stories.summary: The loss of tropical rainforests eased last year, according to new analysis, but scientists warn they are still disappearing rapidly.

stories.headline: Hungary's next PM says frozen EU funds will be paid out soon
stories.summary: Péter Magyar meets EU leaders in Brussels, for the first time since his Tisza party won a landslide election.

stories.headline: United Arab Emirates to quit oil cartel Opec
stories.summary: The UAE's decision, after nearly 60 years of membership, is seen as a potential death knell for the oil cartel.

stories.headline: Faisal Islam: Why the UAE's exit from Opec is a big deal
stories.summary: It will have little effect on the current oil blockades, but it could change everything afterwards.

stories.headline: US soldier accused of betting on Maduro's removal pleads not guilty to fraud charges
stories.summary: The Army officer has been accused of using classified information to win $400,000 on an online prediction market.

stories.headline: Indian billionaire's son offers to save Escobar's hippos
stories.summary: Anant Ambani - son of Asia's richest man Mukesh Ambani - says he can take care of the animals roaming free since the drug lord's death.
